legendOriginOnly.message: Only the origin will be sent.

legendFullUrl.message: Full URL will be sent. ⚠️ May include sensitive data (e.g. tokens, search terms).

helpOverview.message:
What this extension does

This extension allows you to control the sending of Referer headers to websites on a per-domain basis.

This can improve privacy and solve compatibility issues with services that expect specific Referer values.

🏠 Contact

This project is open source and available on GitHub.

GitHub LinkedIn YouTube

helpFunction.message:
How it works

You can assign one of four Referer modes to each domain:

  • 🚫 Send no Referer
  • 🌍 Send only the origin (e.g. https://example.com)
  • 🔗 Send the full URL
  • ♾️ Allow Referer without restriction

Wildcard rules like *.example.com can cover all subdomains. If such a rule is already in place, it will be indicated.

Related Domains

Related domains are automatically detected by the background script. These are domains contacted from the current page, such as:

  • CDNs (e.g. cdn.example.com)
  • APIs (api.example.com)
  • Authentication services (login.partner.com)
  • Third-party content providers

The extension identifies these by observing actual network connections.

🧩 Why related domains are useful

  • Visibility: Shows which domains a page is interacting with.
  • Convenience: Immediately see which domains may require Referer settings to enable full functionality.
  • Quick access: They appear as clickable suggestions in the popup.
  • Consistency: Use the same Referer mode across all involved domains.

🛠 How to use related domains

  1. Open the popup.
  2. Review the list of "Related Domains".
  3. Click on one to insert it into the input field.
  4. Assign a Referer mode to that domain.

helpUseCases.message:
General

  • 🛡️ Prevent websites from seeing where you came from.
  • 🔐 Enable login or download functions that depend on specific referers.
  • 🌐 Define consistent rules for all subdomains of a service.

Login issues

If you have disabled the referer header globally (see 🔧 Tips), some websites may block login even if the page loads fine. This often affects requests to a related domain.

In the following example, login at my.example.com fails unless api.example.com has a suitable referer mode:

In the image below, the referer for api.example.com is set to "🌍 Origin only". The login fields appear:

api.example.com was detected and listed as a "Related Domain" by the extension.

helpPrivacy.message:
Privacy

  • ✅ All settings are stored locally in your browser.
  • ✅ No data is transmitted or synchronised.
  • ✅ No tracking, analytics, or remote requests involved.
  • ⚠️ The "🔗 Send Full URL" mode sends the complete page URL — including its path and query string — to the target domain. Avoid it for domains where the URL could contain sensitive data such as authentication tokens, session IDs, or search terms; prefer "🌍 Send Origin Only" unless the full URL is actually required.

helpTips.message:
Fully disable default Referer

If you want Firefox to stop sending referer headers by default, you can change the following advanced setting:

  1. Enter about:config in the address bar.
  2. Search for network.http.sendRefererHeader.
  3. Set the value to 0.

This extension aims to mimic this setting’s behaviour, but on a per-domain basis instead of globally.

With the referer disabled by default, this extension becomes especially valuable.

Miscellaneous

  • Use *.example.com to cover all subdomains.
  • Existing rules can be edited in the settings.
  • Clicking on a related domain in the popup opens it for editing.
  • If a wildcard rule matches, it is shown and can be modified.
